purpose: Expected SignalEntityLink outputs proving that signals live once and attach to relevant entities through links.
storage_model_principle: Signals live once as independent time-based objects.
Signals attach to every relevant entity through SignalEntityLink.
Entity views may show signals under accounts, people, opportunities, workflows, or other objects without duplicating the underlying signal.

expected_signal_links.evidence_or_matching_rule: Signal source company domain novaworks.example matches canonical Account domain.
Reply came from taylor.morgan@novaworks.example, which resolves to Contact Taylor Morgan.
Contact email domain novaworks.example and source prospect company resolve to Account NovaWorks Labs.
Outreach-like prospect record references seq_out_gtm_001, which resolves to the sequence entity.
Meeting was booked after reply from Contact linked to NovaWorks Account.
Taylor Morgan is the fake meeting participant.
Signal source domain acme.com matches canonical Account domain.
Source reference points to Pioneer Systems record, but signal is stale and low confidence.

expected_no_duplicate_signals.reason: The email reply affects Contact, Account, and Sequence views but must remain one Signal object.
Meeting booked evidence can appear in account and contact views without duplicating the signal.
